project.name: supernode
project.one_liner: Off-chain SuperNode binary for Lumera — storage, replication, metrics, and Everlight participation
project.mission: Run Cascade storage (RaptorQ encode/decode, Kademlia DHT), report LEP-4 compliance metrics, and participate in Everlight retention compensation
success_metrics: SuperNode reports cascade_kademlia_db_bytes accurately via LEP-4 metrics (drives Everlight payout weighting)
STORAGE_FULL state recognized — node continues operating for Sense/Agents AND keeps receiving Everlight payouts for held data
POSTPONED nodes do NOT receive Everlight payouts — this is the key distinction from STORAGE_FULL

in_scope: Everlight SN-1: Add SUPERNODE_STATE_STORAGE_FULL to SDK adapter types
Everlight SN-1: Report cascade_kademlia_db_bytes from P2P database metrics
Everlight SN-1: Allow STORAGE_FULL nodes to start (verifier check)
Everlight SN-1: Consume updated lumera proto types with Everlight fields
out_of_scope: On-chain Everlight module logic (lives in lumera repo)
Everlight Phase 2: LEP-5 challenge-response proof generation by SuperNode
non_goals: Modifying Cascade storage or replication logic
assumptions: lumera repo provides updated proto types with STORAGE_FULL state and Everlight fields
cascade_kademlia_db_bytes can be derived from P2P database size metrics; it drives Everlight payout weighting, not STORAGE_FULL transitions
Multi-repo project: lumera provides protos/chain logic, supernode consumes

must_use: github.com/LumeraProtocol/lumera for chain types (via go.mod replace for local dev)
compliance_security_notes: Metrics must be accurate — inflated cascade_kademlia_db_bytes enables gaming of Everlight payouts (payout weighting metric)
No secrets in code or logs

core_entities: SuperNode: off-chain process running Cascade storage, P2P networking, metrics reporting

SUPERNODE_STATE_STORAGE_FULL: new state triggered on-chain when disk_usage_percent > max_storage_usage_percent (existing param); compute-eligible AND Everlight-payout-eligible for held data. This is the core reason STORAGE_FULL exists: full nodes keep getting paid for stored data, unlike POSTPONED nodes which lose all eligibility.
cascade_kademlia_db_bytes: LEP-4 metric — actual Cascade data held by SuperNode; used for Everlight payout weighting (not STORAGE_FULL trigger)
SupernodeMetrics: collected metrics struct reported to chain via MsgReportSupernodeMetrics
ConfigVerifier: startup checks for SN state validity
state_machine_notes: STORAGE_FULL is determined on-chain by lumera keeper when disk_usage_percent > max_storage_usage_percent (existing param)
STORAGE_FULL SNs are eligible for Everlight storage rewards (distribution pays ACTIVE + STORAGE_FULL proportionally by cascade_kademlia_db_bytes). POSTPONED SNs are NOT eligible.
SuperNode verifier allows startup in ACTIVE, POSTPONED, or STORAGE_FULL states
SuperNode reports metrics; chain decides state transitions

features.name: STORAGE_FULL State in SDK Adapter
features.description: Add SUPERNODE_STATE_STORAGE_FULL constant to sdk/adapters/lumera/types.go and update ParseSupernodeState to recognize it.

features.name: Cascade Kademlia DB Bytes Metric
features.description: Report cascade_kademlia_db_bytes in SupernodeMetrics by reading P2P database size from statusResp.P2PMetrics.Database.P2PDbSizeMb and converting to bytes. This metric is used for Everlight payout weighting (proportional distribution to SNs), not for STORAGE_FULL state transitions.

features.name: Verifier STORAGE_FULL Allowance
features.description: Update ConfigVerifier.checkSupernodeState to allow STORAGE_FULL nodes to start with a warning. Warning should note that STORAGE_FULL nodes keep receiving Everlight payouts for held data, unlike POSTPONED nodes which lose payout eligibility.

features.name: Lumera Proto Dependency Update
features.description: Update go.mod to use lumera version with Everlight proto types (STORAGE_FULL enum, Everlight module types). Currently using local replace directive for development.

acceptance_tests.description: collectMetrics populates CascadeKademliaDbBytes from P2P database size when available
Verifier allows startup when SN is in STORAGE_FULL state (warning, not error)
go build ./... succeeds with updated lumera dependency containing Everlight types

reliability: Metrics collection must not panic if P2PMetrics or Database is nil
security: cascade_kademlia_db_bytes must reflect actual DB size, not be artificially inflatable

external_services: lumera chain: consumes SupernodeMetrics via MsgReportSupernodeMetrics
lumera chain: reads SUPERNODE_STATE from chain via queries

repos.role: blockchain daemon — knowledge provider for protos and chain logic
proof-of-service server layer — consumes lumera chain types
Go SDK — consumes lumera chain types
JS/TS browser SDK — consumes lumera proto definitions via Telescope codegen

cross_repo_contracts.name: Lumera Proto Definitions
cross_repo_contracts.note: lumera proto files are source of truth for supernode chain bindings

cross_repo_contracts.name: SuperNode State Enum
cross_repo_contracts.note: SUPERNODE_STATE_STORAGE_FULL (=6) must match between chain proto and SDK adapter

cross_repo_contracts.name: SupernodeMetrics.cascade_kademlia_db_bytes
cross_repo_contracts.note: Field type double, reported in bytes (converted from MB). Used for Everlight payout weighting, not STORAGE_FULL trigger.

cross_repo_contracts.name: Supernode RewardDistribution Params
cross_repo_contracts.note: Everlight payout params embedded in supernode Params. No standalone x/everlight proto.

integration_acceptance_tests.description: supernode go.mod builds with tagged lumera release containing supernode distribution/Everlight proto updates (no local replace directive)
supernode ParseSupernodeState recognizes SUPERNODE_STATE_STORAGE_FULL (=6) matching lumera proto enum value

recommended_slices.name: Everlight SuperNode Basics
recommended_slices.goal: SuperNode recognizes STORAGE_FULL state, reports cascade_kademlia_db_bytes, and can start in STORAGE_FULL state. Depends on lumera Everlight protos.

open_questions.question: Should supernode branch rebase onto master first to pick up 3 hotfix commits before continuing Everlight work?
When will lumera publish a tagged release with Everlight protos so supernode can drop the local replace directive?

risks.description: Local replace directive (../lumera) in go.mod will break CI/CD
risks.mitigation: Replace with tagged lumera version before merging to master
